choices.text: Δv = Isp × g × ln(m₀/mf). Logarithmic with mass ratio; requires staging. Higher Isp → more delta-v per kg fuel. Chemical limit ~440s; ion ~3000s but low thrust.
LH2 + LOX ~440s (Space Shuttle, SLS, Centaur). Methane+LOX ~330s (Raptor, BE-4). Kerosene+LOX 280-340s (Falcon Merlin). Tradeoff: LH2 low density needs huge tanks.
Electric (ion/Hall) Isp 1,500-4,000s but thrust 50mN-2N. Builds delta-v over months. Deep space (Dawn, BepiColombo) + stationkeeping (Starlink). Useless for launch.
Falcon 9 reused: $2,700/kg vs. Saturn V $50,000/kg = 20× reduction. Starship target $10/kg = 200× more. Same global mass-to-orbit grew 6× since 2010.
Nuclear thermal (Isp ~900s, high thrust) in NASA/DARPA DRACO program. 2× chemical Isp halves fuel for Mars round-trip. Or chemical + Starship architecture for mass to LEO + refueling depot.
